title: Nested-tab deep-linking via nuqs — bookmarkable secondary tabs across the app
scope:
Add URL-encoded deep-link support to every page that uses <SecondaryNavigation>, so that the active secondary tab is reflected in the query string (?tab=<key>), survives page reload, and can be bookmarked or shared.

The implementation follows the architectural recommendation from R040 (Nested Tab Deep-Linking in Next.js 14+ App Router): query-param encoding via the nuqs library (useQueryState + parseAsStringLiteral), history:'replace' with scroll:false on every tab click, a mandatory <Suspense> boundary audit, and a custom lint:tab-deep-link rule to enforce the boundary at CI time.
value:
Secondary tabs currently store their active state in component-local useState. This means:

  • Reloading the page drops the user back to the default tab.
  • Sharing a link to a non-default tab sends the recipient to the wrong place.
  • Browser back/forward does not restore the tab that was open.

Query-param deep-linking solves all three with a single, lightweight convention that requires no backend changes and no new routes.
implementation_plan: Step 1 — Document the nuqs convention (urlKey pattern, Suspense requirement, router.replace semantics) in docs/c_c_secondary_nav_deeplink.md.
Step 2 — Install nuqs, mount NuqsAdapter at app/layout.tsx root; add Vitest smoke test.
Step 3 — Extend SecondaryNavigation with urlKey?: string prop; when present, delegate active/onChange to useQueryState instead of the caller.
Step 4 — Migrate work-items page: remove activeTab useState; pass urlKey='tab'; wrap in Suspense.
Step 5 — Migrate workspace-settings page: pass urlKey='tab'; preserve dirty-state guard; wrap in Suspense.
Step 6 — Migrate theme page: pass urlKey='tab'; wrap in Suspense.
Step 7 — Write lint:tab-deep-link Python script; verify CI exits 0 across all adopters.
areas_impacted: FE-UI: SecondaryNavigation component extended with urlKey prop
FE-DEV: nuqs install, NuqsAdapter root mount, convention doc, lint rule
FE-UX: workspace-settings dirty-state guard preserved through URL migration
feature_list: URL-encoded secondary tab state via nuqs (query-param ?tab=<key>)
Suspense boundary enforcement across all adopters
features_extended: SecondaryNavigation — gains urlKey?: string prop alongside existing pageId (PLA-0014 tab-order); the two props are independent and composable.
work-items page — activeTab useState removed; tabs become bookmarkable.
workspace-settings page — tab deep-link added; existing dirty-state warning preserved.
theme page — tab deep-link added (no dirty-state guard needed).

work_item_backlog.title: Adopt nuqs + nested-tab deep-link convention doc
Adopt deep-link tabs on work-items page
Adopt deep-link tabs on workspace-settings page (dirty-state guard)
Adopt deep-link tabs on theme page

acceptance_criteria.proven_by: file exists and lint:tab-deep-link references it

acceptance_criteria.criterion: nuqs is in package.json and NuqsAdapter is mounted in app/layout.tsx
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: grep confirms import and usage in layout

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Vitest smoke test confirms useQueryState resolves without missing-provider error under NuqsAdapter
Clicking a secondary tab updates the URL to ?tab=<key> via router.replace (no scroll-jump) and reload restores that tab
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: manual browser test on any adopter page

acceptance_criteria.criterion: SecondaryNavigation exposes urlKey?: string; when provided it uses useQueryState with parseAsStringLiteral and history:replace
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: code inspection + TypeScript type check passes

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Vitest contract test verifies URL updates on click, initial render reads ?tab=, unknown values fall back to active prop
useState<'ex1'|'ex2'> removed from work-items/page.tsx; SecondaryNavigation receives urlKey='tab'
Navigating to /work-items?tab=ex1 renders the ex1 panel on first paint without client-side flicker
next build passes with Suspense boundary present on work-items page
Navigating to /workspace-settings?tab=<key> lands on the correct settings panel on first paint
Clicking a tab while the form is dirty triggers the existing unsaved-changes warning before the URL updates; cancelling leaves the URL unchanged

acceptance_criteria.criterion: next build exits 0 after all three adopter pages are wrapped correctly
nuqs appears in package.json dependencies; NuqsAdapter imported and mounted in app/layout.tsx wrapping {children}

risks.risk: Missing Suspense boundary causes next build to fail (CSR bailout)
risks.mitigation: lint:tab-deep-link rule enforces boundary at CI time before merge

risks.risk: NuqsAdapter at root layout may conflict with future SSR-heavy pages that need different query-state handling
risks.mitigation: nuqs is designed for App Router; NuqsAdapter is a thin context provider with no SSR side-effects

risks.risk: workspace-settings dirty-state guard may be bypassed if urlKey fires before onChange
risks.mitigation: urlKey delegates to onChange; nuqs only writes URL after the caller's onChange returns without throwing

references.label: R040 — Nested Tab Deep-Linking in Next.js App Router
PLA-0014 — Tab reorder primitive (pageId prop, dnd-kit, user_tab_order)
nuqs — Type-safe URL state for Next.js
